page: Amazon Web Services g6.12xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor operating at 3.4 GHz, delivering 48 vCPUs across 24 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

For hardware acceleration, the instance bundles four NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L4 GPUs, offering a combined 89 GB of VRAM. Local storage consists of 3760 GB of NVMe SSD capacity. The network interface provides a baseline bandwidth of 40 Gbps. No complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included.

In benchmark testing, the g6.12xlarge exhibits strong performance, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests, Geekbench multi-core, ray tracing, and image processing. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks fall within the average range. Notably, the instance achieves top-tier performance in the top 10% for large language model inference speeds during prompt processing and text generation across small and medium models, as well as prompt processing for large models.

Given its dense configuration of four NVIDIA L4 GPUs and local NVMe storage, the instance offers high resource density for parallel computing tasks. It is highly suited for workloads such as LLM inference, ray tracing, image processing, and graphics-intensive applications.
description: Amazon Web Services g6.12xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance featuring an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 48 vCPUs, 24 physical cores, and 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory. It integrates four NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L4 GPUs with a total of 89 GB VRAM, alongside 3760 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a 40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks indicate strong CPU and GPU performance, with the instance placing in the top 25% for multi-core processing, ray tracing, and image processing. It achieves top-tier results in LLM inference speeds, particularly for prompt processing and text generation on small to medium models. This hardware profile makes the g6.12xlarge highly suitable for graphics-intensive applications, machine learning inference, and parallel computing workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g6.12xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance featuring 48 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, four NVIDIA L4 GPUs with 89 GB VRAM, and 3760 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for intensive parallel workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g6.12xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance with 48 vCPUs, four NVIDIA L4 GPUs, and 3760 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A GPU-accelerated instance featuring four NVIDIA L4 GPUs, dedicated AMD EPYC processors, and high-speed local NVMe SSD storage.
bullet_points: Four NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L4 GPUs with 89 GB total VRAM
AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 48 vCPUs and 192 GB DDR4 RAM
3760 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and 40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Top-tier benchmark performance in LLM inference prompt processing and text generation
Strong multi-core CPU, ray tracing, and image processing capabilities
